title: Excerpt from Last on the List by Amy Daws
text: Last on the List by Amy Daws Chapter 44 Weeks Pregnant: 24 Animals on the Mountain: 10 Trista My face heats as Wyatt drags me out of the barn back toward the party. He looks like a kid on Christmas morning as he pulls me in close, tucking me under his arm, clearly ready to out our relationship status without the least bit of hesitation. I clear my throat and squeeze his arm nervously. “Not to be, like, ‘that girl’ or anything, but we’re walking back to this party in a very different position than we arrived and I don’t know what people are going to think.” Wyatt’s twinkling eyes narrow on me. “I thought I told you I don’t really care what people think.” “I know, but these are your family and closest friends, so... I care.” I swallow the knot in my throat and glance nervously around at everyone’s eyes on us. “What are we exactly?” Wyatt’s brows lift curiously. “I guess that’s your call.” “My call?” My voice turns shrill as we move closer and closer to the guests milling around. “What do you mean, my call?” Wyatt stops in his tracks and turns his back on everyone to look me in the eye. “I’ve put myself out there with you already, Trista. You know where my head is and how I feel about you. But I’m not going to rush you into something you’re not ready for. If you want space for the rest of this shower so we can figure out what we are later...then I’ll give you space.” He steps back and I instantly feel the loss of him, my anxiety ratcheting up even higher with the growing distance between us. He crosses his arms expectantly, and my mind begins to spin with all the possible scenarios at play here. Everyone here knows me as Trista the Surrogate. Trista the Incubator having the grumpy mountain man’s baby. Now I’m Trista the... Butterflies erupt in my belly over the new potential labels I could give myself. I could give us. The possibilities give me a new perspective on life, one that I barely let myself consider before. I lick my lips and smile up at Mr. Mountain Man. His trim beard, his fresh button-down, his grin—it all looks divinely good on him. Stepping forward, I reach out and take his hand in mine. “No space, please.” I tip my head back and offer my lips to him, and he kindly obliges. I hear a faint gasp and murmurings coming from all around us, and I decide to go full send and wrap my hands around his neck to deepen the kiss. His husky laugh tastes like the hope that I’d scarcely allowed myself to have in my life. As we join the party, I gird my fruitful loins for some harsh judgment. There might even be some looks of disgust...a surrogate and her intended parent outing their relationship isn’t exactly conventional. But as I look from face to face, all I see are smiles. Genuine, knowing and maybe even somewhat smug smiles. His mother even winks at me. They all knew. Every damn one of them. I don’t know if Wyatt told them or if we weren’t as good at keeping our secret as I thought. But this family I found out here doesn’t seem the least bit surprised by our change in relationship status. A loud throat-clearing has Wyatt and I separating from another kiss with silly, dopey smiles. I look over and see Calder standing there with a skeptical expression on his face. “Does this mean I can start calling you Mama Bear?” His brows waggle knowingly. My heart lurches as I press my hand to my belly and turn toward my mountain man. “I might insist on it.”
